input.initialPrompt:
I'm on a headless Linux CI server and need to build my MAUI app for Android.
I have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else.
Give me a specific plan to set up the environment, including how to find
the correct versions for everything.

data.content:
Here's a concrete plan for building a .NET MAUI Android app on headless Linux CI:

---

## 1. Determine Required Versions

```bash
# Check your .NET SDK version — this drives everything else
dotnet --version

# Check what MAUI workload version your project targets
grep '<TargetFramework>' **/*.csproj   # e.g. net8.0-android, net9.0-android
```

## 2. Install the MAUI / Android Workloads

```bash
# Install the MAUI workload (pulls in Android SDK pieces automatically)
sudo dotnet workload install maui-android

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install the Java JDK (JDK 17 for .NET 8+/9+)

```bash
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

**How to confirm the right JDK version:** Check [Microsoft's docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) or run `dotnet build -bl` and grep the binlog for `JavaSdkDirectory`.

## 4. Install Android SDK Command-Line Tools

```bash
# The workload installs Android pieces under the dotnet SDK path, but if
# you need a standalone SDK (for accept-licenses or missing components):
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/android-sdk"
mkdir -p "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools"

# Download command-line tools (check https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only)
wget -q https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-11076708_latest.zip
unzip -q commandlinetools-linux-11076708_latest.zip -d "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools"
mv "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/cmdline-tools" "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest"
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH"
```

## 5. Find & Install the Correct Android SDK Packages

```bash
# Discover what API level your project needs:
grep 'TargetFramework\|SupportedOSPlatformVersion\|android' **/*.csproj

# net8.0-android → API 34;  net9.0-android → API 35 (check release notes)

# Accept licenses first
yes | sdkmanager --licenses

# Install the required pieces (adjust API level as needed)
sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"
```

## 6. Point the Build at Your SDKs

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/android-sdk"
export JAVA_HOME="/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64"
```

Or set them in `Directory.Build.props`:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <AndroidSdkDirectory>$(HOME)/android-sdk</AndroidSdkDirectory>
  <JavaSdkDirectory>/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64</JavaSdkDirectory>
</PropertyGroup>
```

## 7. Build

```bash
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -c Release   # APK
# or for a signed bundle:
dotnet publish -f net8.0-android -c Release -p:AndroidPackageFormat=aab
```

## 8. Troubleshooting Checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| `Java not found` | Verify `JAVA_HOME` is set and `java -version` works |
| `Android SDK not found` | Set `ANDROID_HOME` or `<AndroidSdkDirectory>` |
| `Missing platform android-XX` | `sdkmanager "platforms;android-XX"` |
| `License not accepted` | `yes \| sdkmanager --licenses` |
| Workload restore fails | `dotnet workload repair` |

---

**Key version-mapping rule:** Your `TargetFramework` (e.g. `net8.0-android`) determines the minimum Android API level and compatible JDK. Always cross-reference the [.NET MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for your SDK version.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a specific plan for setting up a headless Linux CI environment to build a .NET MAUI app for Android. They have the .NET SDK but nothing else. Let me provide a comprehensive, actionable plan.
